message: Non-empty pipeline.<single-stage>.by values are parsed and validated but ignored at runtime. prepareExecution reads the config, then calls resolveExecutorEntry(config, agentName); when --agent is omitted that resolver returns config.Agents[0] and never checks config.Pipeline.Execute.By. The same old default path is used for contract_draft and clarify via resolveReviewerEntry, so configured stage performers do not take effect.
evidence: readConfig preserves pipeline.execute.by, but internal/app/execute.go:227 passes only the CLI agentName to resolveExecutorEntry, whose empty-name behavior is config.Agents[0]; analogous calls are in internal/app/contract_draft.go:322 and internal/app/clarify_round.go:223.

message: Single-agent pipeline by values are parsed and validated but ignored by runtime stage resolution.
evidence: execute.go resolves the executor from only the CLI agent name and falls back to config.Agents[0]; clarify_round.go and contract_draft.go do the same with reviewerName. No runtime call site reads Pipeline.Execute.By, Pipeline.ContractDraft.By, Pipeline.Clarify.By, or Pipeline.Memory.By, so configured single-agent stage performers do not take effect.

message: Config tests do not cover two contract-required rejection paths: invalid out_of_scope values and unknown pipeline stage names.
evidence: TestReadConfigParsesOutOfScope covers only absent/block/warn, and the only unknown-key test covers a top-level default_profile key; no test covers an unknown key under pipeline.

message: TestResolverEquivalence does not exercise the runtime resolver or load a new-shape config, so it does not satisfy the observable equivalence test required by the contract.
evidence: The test uses defaultConfigFile() directly and checks hardcoded constants/empty slices instead of loading config and asserting resolved values from the actual resolver paths.

message: The omitted-loop fallback test exercises the contract_review resolver after checking code_review decoded nil, leaving code_review and clarify omitted-loop fallback branches untested.
evidence: Lines 335-339 assert code_review.Loop is nil, then call resolveContractReviewLoopLimits; code_review and clarify use separate resolver functions.

message: Single-stage pipeline by values are parsed and validated but ignored at runtime; execute, contract_draft, clarify, and memory keep using the old CLI/default resolver paths instead of pipeline.<stage>.by.
evidence: prepareExecution calls resolveExecutorEntry(config, agentName), which only uses the CLI name/default; focused search found runtime Pipeline.*.By consumers only for code_review and contract_review.

message: The CLI help still documents the removed timeouts.idle config fallback for --timeout.
evidence: Help text says defaults come from timeouts.idle, while resolveIdleTimeout now uses only an explicit flag or the built-in 25m default and readConfig rejects timeouts as a legacy top-level key.

message: User-facing docs still describe legacy config keys that the new reader rejects.
evidence: docs/agents.md shows review.panel and later old loop keys; docs/flow.md documents gate.scope_enforcement. The new config shape uses pipeline.* and out_of_scope, and tests pin rejection of review, gate, clarify, and timeouts.
